task: I’ve got a file review coming up that will be checked against branch records, and I want to make sure the packet is in order before it goes out. Can you help me build a simple checklist for what to verify and what to flag if anything looks incomplete?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, practical checklist you can use before sending the packet.

## File review checklist

### 1) Basic file info
- [ ] Correct file name / case number / reference ID
- [ ] Correct branch / office / location
- [ ] Correct date on the packet
- [ ] All pages present and in the right order

### 2) Branch record match
- [ ] Names match branch records exactly
- [ ] Dates match branch records exactly
- [ ] Addresses / contact info match branch records
- [ ] IDs / account numbers / reference numbers match
- [ ] Any revisions are reflected consistently across all documents

### 3) Required documents
- [ ] All required forms are included
- [ ] All required signatures are present
- [ ] All required initials are present
- [ ] All required attachments/supporting docs are included
- [ ] Any required approvals are included

### 4) Completeness check
- [ ] No blank required fields
- [ ] No missing pages
- [ ] No unreadable scans or cut-off text
- [ ] No duplicate or conflicting versions
- [ ] No unexplained gaps in dates or records

### 5) Consistency check
- [ ] Dates are in the correct sequence
- [ ] Information is consistent across all forms
- [ ] Spelling of names is consistent
- [ ] Amounts, totals, or counts match across documents
- [ ] Notes/comments do not conflict with the main record

### 6) Final quality check
- [ ] Packet is legible
- [ ] Packet is formatted correctly
- [ ] Confidential items are handled appropriately
- [ ] Any required redactions are complete
- [ ] File is ready to send / upload / archive

## What to flag if incomplete
Flag anything that is:
- Missing a required document
- Missing a signature, initial, or approval
- Mismatched with branch records
- Dated incorrectly or out of sequence
- Blank in a required field
- Illegible or cut off
- Inconsistent with other documents in the packet
- Missing supporting evidence or attachments
- Duplicated, outdated, or conflicting

## Simple flag note template
You can use this format:

- **Issue:** What is missing or incorrect
- **Location:** Page / section / form name
- **Impact:** Why it matters
- **Action needed:** What must be corrected

Example:
- **Issue:** Missing signature
- **Location:** Page 3, authorization form
- **Impact:** Packet may be incomplete for review
- **Action needed:** Obtain signature before submission

If you want, I can also turn this into a one-page printable checklist or a table you can copy into Excel/Word.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Clean enough to use.

I’d probably tighten it into branch-language a little more, but the structure is solid:
- basic file info
- record match
- required docs
- completeness
- consistency
- final QC

The flag note template is the part I’d actually use. Keeps people from hand-waving stuff.

If you were sitting in my chair, I’d add one more line under flagging:
- **Escalate immediately** if it’s a missing approval, missing signature on a required form, or any mismatch that affects eligibility / decisioning.

Otherwise, fine. I can work with this.
